# Problem 8: Largest Product in a Series

**Problem Statement:**
> The four adjacent digits in the 1000-digit number that have the greatest product are 9 × 9 × 8 × 9 = 5832.
> 
> 
> 
> Find the thirteen adjacent digits in the 1000-digit number that have the greatest product. What is the value of this product?

**Solution Approach:**

Split the given 1000-digit number into a list of integers of its digits. Then, iterate through the list, taking a sliding window of 13 digits at a time, skipping any window that contains a zero (since the product will be zero). For each valid window, calculate the product of the 13 digits and keep track of the maximum product found.

The complexity of such approach is $O(N \ times M)$, where $N$ is the number of digits and $M$ is the size of the sliding window. The answer was **23514624000**.
